headline: Token economics broke out across the quarter as the agent tooling stack that led prior windows cooled.
body: A cluster around cost and compression surfaced as new entrants and breakouts: tokenomics, token usage analysis, token efficiency, model compression, quantization, and CLI. This is reinforced by reporting on a possible "Tokenpocalypse" of rising token prices tied to AI IPO strategies [0], alongside the rise of LLM as a breakout concept amid labor anxiety from engineers who say LLMs are eroding their careers [9]. Meanwhile the agent tooling stack that dominated earlier, agent orchestration, MCP, LSP, subagents, skills, context management, and code navigation, all weakened together. Smaller threads include OpenAI's reported "super app" and a senior view that "chat is dead" [4], and renewed alignment and control debate [7].

cited_items.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
Will artificial intelligence soon escape human control? - The Economist
